---
title: "About"
---


![Vince Pileggi, Ph.D., P.Eng.](images/MeInAfrica.png){width=200px}

Hello! I'm Vince Pileggi, a wastewater engineering expert, with the Ontario Ministry fo the Environment, Conservation and Parks and adjunct professor at the University of Guelph, School of Engineering.

Aside from my regular work I'm particularly passionate about the correct application of statistics to solving engineering problems when appropriate. I use VS Code [@VSCode], Python [@Python], GitHub [@GitHub], RStudio [@rstudio] for technical publishing and of course $\LaTeX$ [@latex] for high-quality typesetting.

My dissertation was focused on municipal sludge treatment using an anaerobic membrane bioreactor and is available though my Research Gate profile [@pileggi_researchgate]. The thesis was written in $LaTeX$ but included as significant amount of R-coding and statistics. It was a lot of fun with some frustration finishing it off 😄! 


This project hosts statistical analyses and technical notes.
Built with **Quarto** and published via **GitHub Pages** (Option A, `/docs` on `main`).

## Guiding Principle

This website is dedicated to advancing environmental engineering, applied statistics, and reproducible technical reporting through the sharing of public knowledge and educational resources.

Content is intentionally presented in a generalized form and emphasizes engineering methods, statistical principles, and reproducible workflows rather than specific projects or assessments.

Accordingly, this website does not include or disclose:

- confidential assessments;
- facility, client, municipality, or project names;
- suppliers, manufacturers, or technology proponents;
- proprietary information;
- internal analyses or regulatory reviews; or
- unpublished project-specific data.

Any examples presented are illustrative and are based on public information, synthetic datasets, or generalized engineering concepts developed for educational and professional development purposes.


## References
